title: 3 OAS Access and Administration
body_text:
3 	OAS Access and Administration
3.1	Overview of OAS Access and Administration
Access Levels
OAS is a Client Centre hosted application that requires two levels of access to log in:
Client Centre access using a Client Centre User ID having OAS access permission
Granting OAS application access is described in the following sections:
â
Section 3.2, Granting OAS CSR Access to a Client Centre User
â
Section 3.3, Granting OAS User Administration Access to a User Who is Currently a CPS Admin Security Officer
â
Section 3.4, Granting OAS User Administration Access to a User Who is a New CPS Admin Security Officer
OAS application access using an OAS User ID
Setting up OAS application user accounts is described in Section 3.6, Managing OAS User Accounts â OAS User Administrators, Section 3.7, Managing OAS User Accounts â OAS CSRs, and Section 3.8, Managing OAS User Accounts â OAS Fraud Analysts.
OAS User Types
There are three types of OAS users, as described below:
OAS User Administrator
OAS User Administrators can perform the following functions in OAS:
â
Create and view OAS user accounts
â
Reset OAS usersâ passwords
â
Enable/Disable OAS user accounts
â
Delete OAS user accounts
Note:	OAS User Administrator access is limited to performing OAS user management functions. They canât search or view customer profiles or transfers.
OAS CSR (Customer Service Representative)
OAS CSRs can search and view the following details in OAS:
â
Status of your customersâ
Interac
e-Transfer
Â®
transactions (e.g., Blocked, Active, Received)
â
Your customersâ
Interac
e-Transfer profile details (name, email address, etc.)
â
Your customersâ
Interac
e-Transfer history (transaction history, creation date, etc.)
â
Your customersâ
Interac
e-Transfer Autodeposit registrations
Note:	OAS CSRs can only search or view customer profiles and transfers. They canât search or view OAS user accounts.
OAS Fraud Analyst
OAS Fraud Analysts can manage
Interac
e-Transfer fraud incidents using the following OAS functionality:
â
View reported fraud-suspicious transactions
â
View additional transaction details for fraud-suspicious transactions (including counterparty IP address information for non-Autodeposit
Interac
e-Transfer transactions, and payment memos/references)
â
Initiate or respond to funds recovery requests
â
View another financial institutionâs fraud analyst investigation notes on
Interac
e-Transfer transactions or funds recovery updates
â
Cancel or unblock a pending
Interac
e-Transfer transaction
â
Disable or enable an
Interac
e-Transfer customer profile
Note:	The OAS Fraud Analyst role is only available to financial institutions that execute an agreement with Central 1 as to the terms and conditions governing the use of this role. Only Central 1 can assign the OAS Fraud Analyst role to a financial institution user, upon request from the financial institution. The financial institution user must be an existing OAS CRS to be promoted to the OAS Fraud Analyst role.
Note:	Financial Institutions interested in implementing the OAS Fraud Analyst role must email Central 1âs FAS team and request to be approved for this role. FAS coordinates with them for the execution and return of a Terms of Use agreement. Requests for promoting individual OAS CSR Users to the role of OAS Fraud Analyst must be sent to the FAS team after your institution is approved for the use of this role. See Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources, for FAS contact information.
Note:	Financial institution users granted the OAS Fraud Analyst role can investigate and action customer profiles and transfers, but they canât access OAS user accounts.
3.2	Granting OAS CSR Access to a Client Centre User
A CPS Admin Security Officer can assign OAS access to users via CPS Admin or User Management. This Section includes procedures for assigning OAS access via CPS Admin. For information on how to assign OAS access via User Management, refer to the User Management Part.
Note: 	CPS Admin Security Officers can only assign access to OAS CSR users. OAS User Administrators must be CPS Admins.
CPS Admin Security Officers proceed as follows to assign OAS CSR user access via CPS Admin:
Log in to CPS Admin, then select
Click here
. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: Accessing CPS Admin
Important
:	The CPS Admin Security Officer must have permission to assign OAS access to Client Centre users.
If the user is already set up as a Client Centre user, select
Edit User
to open their Client Centre profile. Refer to the CPS Admin Application Part for procedures on locating and modifying Client Centre users.
If the user isnât yet set up as a Client Centre user, select
Add User
and create the Client Centre user. Refer to the CPS Admin Application Part for procedures on adding Client Centre users.
After the Client Centre userâs profile is displayed, scroll down to the Applications Menu and check the box beside âOAS_eTransfersâ. Select
Submit
at the bottom of the page. See Figure 2.
Figure 2: Granting OAS Access in CPS Admin
Ask another CPS Admin Security Officer to authorize the task.
3.3	Granting OAS User Administrator Access to a User Who is Currently a CPS Admin Security Officer
Proceed as follows to set up an OAS User Administrator on CPS Admin if the individual is already a CPS Admin Security Officer:
Complete the CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request, Form 2035, as described in the CPS Admin Application Part. See Figure 3.
Completion of the form is self-explanatory, but note the following:
â
Change Request Details â Personal Access Rights
Record âOAS User Administratorâ in this section if the CPS Admin Security Officer is to be given personal access to OAS.
â¡
Change Request Details â Administrative Rights
Record âOAS User Administratorâ in this section if the CPS Admin Security Officer is to give access to OAS to other users.
â¢
Financial Institution Authorization
Ensure that the form is signed by two signing officers on file with your central credit union.
Forward the form to your central credit union, as indicated on page 2. If applicable, the central credit union authorizes the form and forwards it to Central 1.
Figure 3: CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request, Form 2035
Note:	Central 1 assigns OAS application access to the CPS Admin Security Officer on receipt of the completed Form 2035.
Related Information
CPS Admin Modification/Deletion Request,
Form 2035
3.4	Granting OAS User Administrator Access to a User Who is a New CPS Admin Security Officer
Proceed as follows to set up an OAS User Administrator if the individual is a new CPS Admin Security Officer:
Complete the CPS Admin Appointment Certificate, Form 1918, as described in the CPS Admin Application Part. See Figure 4.
Completion of the form is self-explanatory, but note the following:
â
Request Details â Personal Access Rights
Record âOAS User Administratorâ in this section if the CPS Admin Security Officer is to be given personal access to OAS.
â¡
Request Details â Administrative Rights
Record âOAS User Administratorâ in this section if the CPS Admin Security Officer is to give OAS access to other users.
â¢
Financial Institution Authorization
Ensure that the form is signed by two signing officers on file with your central credit union.
Forward the form to your central credit union as indicated on page 2. If applicable, the central credit union authorizes the form and forwards it to Central 1.
Note:	Central 1 assigns OAS application access to the CPS Admin Security Officer on receipt of the completed Form 1918.
Figure 4: CPS Admin Appointment Certificate, Form 1918
Central 1 communicates a unique one-time password to new CPS Admin Security Officers.
Related Information
CPS Admin Appointment Certificate,
Form 1918
3.5	Logging in to OAS
To log in to OAS:
Go to
https://clients.central1.com
and enter your Client Centre username and password. See Figure 5.
Figure 5: Client Centre Login
Select
Applications
. See Figure 6.
Figure 6: Accessing Applications on the Client Centre
Select
Interac
e-Transfer
Â®
OAS
. See Figure 7.
Note: 	Applications are grouped by type (e.g., Admin & Support, Payments). If needed, scroll down the page to find the application. If youâve saved the application as a favourite, it will also appear under âMy Favouritesâ.
Figure 7: Accessing OAS
Enter your OAS User ID and password on the OAS login page, then select
Log In
. See Figure 8.
Figure 8: OAS Log In Screen
Note:	During their first login to OAS, users are prompted to change their password.
The OAS home page differs according to the OAS user type. For example, when OAS User Administrators log in to OAS, they have access to OAS user management functions.
See Section 1.1, Overview of the
Interac
e-Transfer
Online Administration System (OAS), for a summary of OAS application features available.
For an OAS User Administrator, OAS opens on the Users screen where OAS user management tasks are performed. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: OAS User Administrator Home Screen
For an OAS CSR, OAS opens on a Home screen providing access to
Interac
e-Transfer transaction and customer profile search functions. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: OAS CSR User Home Screen
For an OAS Fraud Analyst, OAS opens on a Case Management screen providing access to outgoing
Interac
e-Transfer transactions currently reported as potentially fraudulent. See Figure 11. Refer to Chapter 6, The OAS Fraud Analyst Role, for a description of using OAS as an OAS Fraud Analyst.
Figure 11: OAS Fraud Analyst User Case Management Screen
3.6	Managing OAS User Accounts â OAS User Administrators
Creating an OAS User Administrator Account
For OAS User Administrators, Central 1 creates an OAS user account for the individual at the same time as they grant OAS application access, on receipt of a completed Form 1918 or Form 2035. Completion of these forms is described Sections 3.3, Granting OAS Access to a User Who is Currently a CPS Admin Security Officer, and Section 3.4, Granting OAS Access to a User Who is a New CPS Admin Security Officer.
Central 1 creates the OAS user account for the individual as an OAS User Administrator and communicates to them their OAS login credentials, including their OAS User ID and one-time password.
Note:	The user must log in to OAS within 24 hours or the one-time password expires, and the user is locked out. The user is prompted at their first login to change their password.
Managing an OAS User Administrator Account
To modify, delete, enable, or disable, or reset the password for an OAS User Administrator account, contact Central 1.
3.7	Managing OAS User Accounts â OAS CSRs
Creating an OAS CSR
An OAS User Administrator proceeds as follows to create an OAS CSR user account:
Log in to OAS as described in Section 3.5, Logging in to OAS.
Select
Add User
. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Selecting Add User in OAS
Complete the Add New User screen. Completion of this page is self-explanatory, except as noted below. See Figure 13.
Figure 13: The Add New User screen
Note:
Interac
stores all OAS User IDs for all financial institutions that subscribe to OAS in a single repository. To avoid any potential duplication of OAS User IDs, when assigning an OAS User ID for your users, follow a naming convention that includes using a standard identifier for your financial institution as a prefix to the username. For example, CUNAMEJDOE. The OAS User ID isnât case sensitive.
Take note of the userâs password and communicate the OAS User ID and one-time password to the user in a secure and confidential manner.
Select
Save
.
Note:	The OAS CSR must log in within 24 hours, or the password expires and the user is locked out. The user is prompted on their first login to change their password.
Editing an OAS User Account
An OAS User Administrator proceeds as follows to edit an OAS user account:
Log in to OAS as described in Section 3.5, Logging in to OAS.
Select the appropriate user from the list. See Figure 14.
Figure 14: Selecting a User
Modify the user details as needed, then select
Save
. See Figure 15.
Figure 15: Modifying a User
Note:	An OAS User Administrator canât modify the user account of another OAS User Administrator. To modify the user details of an OAS User Administrator account, contact Central 1 for assistance.
Enabling/Disabling an OAS User Account
An OAS User Administrator proceeds as follows to enable or disable an OAS user account:
Log in to OAS as described in Section 3.5, Logging in to OAS.
Select the appropriate user from the list. See Figure 16.
Figure 16: Selecting a User
For each OAS CSR user displayed in the List of OAS Users, in the Action column there is an action that displays the current enabled/disabled state of the user account. When a profile is disabled. the action is colored red and is labelled âDisabledâ; when a profile is enabled, the action is colored green and labelled âEnabledâ. The same action is also seen in the Edit User screen when a user account is selected for editing.
Selecting this action toggles the state of the user account between the two states of enabled and disabled: to enable a disabled profile, select
Disabled
; to disable an enabled profile, select
Enabled
. See Figure 17.
Figure 17: Enabling an OAS User
Select
Enable
to confirm the change. See Figure 18.
Figure 18: The OAS Enable User Confirmation Prompt
Note:	An OAS User Administrator canât enable or disable the account of another OAS User Administrator. To enable or disable an OAS User Administrator account, contact Central 1.
Resetting an OAS User Account Password
An OAS User Administrator proceeds as follows to reset an OAS user account password:
Log in to OAS as described in Section 3.5, Logging in to OAS.
Select the appropriate user from the list. See Figure 19.
Figure 19: Selecting a User from the List of OAS Users
Select
Reset Password
. See Figure 20.
Figure 20: Selecting Reset Password
From the Reset Password screen, record the userâs temporary password, then select
Reset Password
to confirm the password reset. See Figure 21.
Figure 21: Confirming the Password Reset
Communicate the one-time password to the user in a secure and confidential manner.
Note:	The OAS User must log in within 24 hours, or the password expires and the user is locked out. The user is prompted to change their password when they log in to OAS. See Enabling/Disabling an OAS User Account above for more information.
Note:	Login to OAS is a two-step process. The user first logs in to the Client Centre, selects OAS as the application they wish to open, and then enters their OAS credentials on the OAS login screen. It is only after successfully providing both sets of credentials at both application login screens that the user gains access to OAS. The userâs Client Centre credentials are separate and distinct from their OAS credentials. Re-setting an OAS user account password as described above doesn't affect a userâs Client Centre user account password, or vice versa. Changing user credentials in one system doesn't affect the user credentials in the other.
Note:	An OAS User Administrator canât reset the password for the account of another OAS User Administrator. To reset the password for an OAS User Administrator account, contact Central 1.
Deleting an OAS User Account
An OAS User Administrator proceeds as follows to delete an OAS user account:
Log in to OAS as described in Section 3.5, Logging in to OAS.
Select the appropriate user from the list. See Figure 22.
Figure 22: Selecting an OAS User from the User List
Select
Delete user
. See Figure 23.
Figure 23: Deleting an OAS User
Select
Delete
to confirm the deletion. See Figure 24.
Figure 24: The Confirm Delete User Prompt
Note:	An OAS User Administrator canât delete the account of another OAS User Administrator. To delete the account of an OAS User Administrator, contact Central 1.
3.8	Managing OAS User Accounts â OAS Fraud Analysts
Central 1 will promote an OAS CSR user to the OAS Fraud Analyst role upon request from your financial institution. However, the OAS Fraud Analyst role is only available if your financial institution has entered into an agreement with Central 1 for the use of this role.
Note:	Financial institutions interested in implementing the OAS Fraud Analyst role must email Central 1âs FAS team and request to be approved for this role. FAS coordinates with them for the execution and return of a Terms of Use agreement. Requests for promoting individual OAS CSR Users to the role of OAS Fraud Analyst must be sent to the FAS team after your institution is approved for the use of this role. See Section 1.2, Contacts, Websites, and Other Resources for FAS contact information.
END
